steps.title: Locate the clause boundary
steps.content: The sentence contrasts ordinary beige sand with Tangsi Beach's unusual shade, then gives a second independent clause explaining that crushed coral and organic matter create the pink hue.

steps.title: Set off the transition
steps.content: Choice B leaves the two independent clauses joined by only a comma. Choice C lacks punctuation after though, and Choice D puts the semicolon before though, leaving the following words dependent. A comma sets off though, and a semicolon separates the clauses. The answer is A.
